checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=48,946,718.07 (2009 annual) -> NetIncomeLoss=93,373,697.22 (2010 annual), +90.7% -> NetIncomeLoss=134,151,698.34 (2011 annual), +43.7%

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=39,623,571.30 (2009-12-31) -> InventoryNet=52,081,299.24 (2011-12-31), roughly proportionate to revenue growth of ~24% over same period

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly values range from 9,776,700.99 (Q1 2010) to 44,663,238.23 (Q3 2011), showing substantial quarter-to-quarter variability rather than unusual smoothness

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=10,910,586.54 (2009-12-31) -> 10,957,206.22 (2010-12-31), +0.4% -> 11,448,688.32 (2011-12-31), +4.5%, while ReceivablesNetCurrent grew +2.7% (2009-2010) and +22.7% (2010-2011); allowance/receivables ratio fell from 2.31% (2009) to 1.92% (2011)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-02-16, followed by 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 restating multiple years (2008-2011) of Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, AccountsPayableCurrent, Depreciation, InterestExpense, SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpense, and cash flow data

Multiple CORRESP and UPLOAD filings in 2011-2012 (e.g., CORRESP 2011-06-08, 2011-06-28, 2011-07-08, 2011-08-26, 2012-06-07; UPLOAD 2011-06-06, 2011-07-26, 2011-09-02, 2012-05-14, 2012-06-15) indicating an active SEC comment-letter review process around the time of the restatement

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spans from 2007-12-31 through 2012-06-30 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, ReceivablesNetCurrent, Goodwill, AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent, and operating cash flow, providing roughly 4.5 years of trend data plus a full filing chronology back to 1994

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-provisioning of bad debt allowance relative to rapidly growing receivables (allowance/receivables ratio fell from 2.31% in 2009 to 1.92% in 2011 while receivables grew 22.7% vs allowance growth of only 4.5%), combined with receivables outpacing revenue growth, suggests possible aggressive revenue recognition or insufficient reserve build that inflates reported receivables and net income.

Filing of a 10-K/A only ~1 month after the original 10-K, restating Goodwill (+64% in Q4 2010), Intangibles (+93% in Q4 2010), and multiple income statement/cash flow line items for FY2008-2011, indicates a prior material misstatement (likely related to acquisition purchase-price allocation or error correction) that was subsequently corrected.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-02-16; 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 restating Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsPayableCurrent, Depreciation, InterestExpense for FY2008-2011

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Net income grew far faster than operating cash flow (NI +90.7% in 2010 and +43.7% in 2011 vs OCF growth of only +4.4% and +1.4%), suggesting increasing reliance on non-cash accruals (e.g., receivables growth, deferred cost recognition) to drive reported earnings growth rather than cash-backed profitability.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K/A restated cash flow and income statement

top_signals: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 restating multiple years (2008-2011) of Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, Goodwill, Intangibles, AP, and cash flow data just one month after the original 10-K, alongside repeated SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence in 2011-2012
Allowance for doubtful accounts grew only 4.5% (2010-2011) while receivables grew 22.7%, causing reserve coverage to decline from 2.31% to 1.92% of receivables
Net income growth (+90.7% in 2010, +43.7% in 2011) substantially outpaced operating cash flow growth (+4.4%, +1.4%), indicating rising accrual-based earnings
Goodwill and intangible assets jumped 64% and 93% respectively in a single quarter (Q4 2010) as later restated, indicating possible purchase accounting issues
